name: Workforce Solutions for Tarrant County
eligibility: Open to all jobseekers; income-eligible for WIOA training vouchers
notes: TWC anchor for Tarrant County. Always relevant for jobseekers. Reentry navigators on site.

name: Texas Workforce Commission - Online Portal
eligibility: All Texas residents
notes: Statewide TWC services. File unemployment, search jobs, find training programs.

name: Goodwill Industries Career Services
eligibility: Open to all jobseekers; reentry program for justice-involved
notes: Free career classes, computer training, reentry support. Always relevant.

name: Fort Worth Public Library Career Resources
eligibility: Free to public
notes: Free WiFi, computer terminals, career database access (LearningExpress, Brainfuse).

name: Trinity Metro Reduced Fare Program
address: 1600 E Lancaster Ave, Fort Worth, TX 76102
eligibility: Income-eligible (200% FPL), seniors 65+, persons with disabilities
notes: Reduced bus + TEXRail fare program. Apply with proof of income or disability.

name: Trinity Railway Express (TRE)
eligibility: Open to all riders
notes: Commuter rail Fort Worth to Dallas via 10 stations. Transit-friendly for DFW commute.

name: Catholic Charities Fort Worth - Car Repair Grants
address: 249 W Thornhill Dr, Fort Worth, TX 76115
eligibility: Income-eligible Tarrant County residents
notes: Up to $1500 for emergency car repairs needed for work. Application via case manager.

name: MITS - Mobility Impaired Transportation Service
address: 1600 E Lancaster Ave, Fort Worth, TX 76102
eligibility: ADA-eligible riders unable to use fixed-route bus
notes: ADA paratransit service for riders unable to use fixed-route bus. Application required.

name: TWC Child Care Services - Tarrant County
eligibility: Working/training parents at or below 85% State Median Income
notes: Income-based childcare subsidy. Pays directly to TRS-rated providers. 12-month enrollment.

name: Camp Fire First Texas
eligibility: Open to all; subsidies accepted
notes: Texas Rising Star 4-star center. Accepts TWC subsidies. Multiple Tarrant locations.

name: Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Fort Worth
address: 3218 E Belknap St, Fort Worth, TX 76111
eligibility: Ages 6-18, sliding scale by income
notes: After-school care at multiple Fort Worth and Tarrant locations. Sliding-scale fees.

name: Head Start of Greater Fort Worth
eligibility: Income-eligible families with children ages 3-5
notes: Federally-funded preschool for income-eligible families. Free.

name: ChildCareGroup
eligibility: Tarrant and Dallas county residents
notes: Texas Rising Star resource center; helps navigate childcare search and subsidy.

name: Legal Aid of NorthWest Texas
address: 600 E Weatherford St, Fort Worth, TX 76102
eligibility: Income-eligible (125% FPL); civil legal matters
notes: Free civil legal services. Record clearing eligibility consults.

name: Texas RioGrande Legal Aid - Reentry
eligibility: Income-eligible Texas residents with criminal records
notes: Statewide reentry legal services. Phone consults for Tarrant County residents.

name: Cornbread Hustle - Reentry Job Board
eligibility: Justice-involved jobseekers (felony OK)
notes: Reentry-focused job board with vetted fair-chance employers across DFW.

name: Tarrant County Reentry Services
address: 200 Taylor St 9th Floor, Fort Worth, TX 76196
eligibility: Tarrant County residents with criminal records
notes: Tarrant County reentry navigators. Free case management for justice-involved.

name: Cassata Catholic High School - Adult Education
eligibility: Open to adults completing high school or GED, including reentry students
notes: Reentry-friendly accelerated diploma program. Sliding-scale tuition.

name: JPS Health Network - Sliding Scale Clinics
address: 1500 S Main St, Fort Worth, TX 76104
eligibility: Tarrant County residents; sliding-scale based on income
notes: Tarrant County's public hospital network. Sliding-scale fees, JPS Connection card available.

name: MHMR of Tarrant County
eligibility: Tarrant County residents with mental health or IDD needs
notes: Local Mental Health Authority. Crisis line: 817-335-3022.

name: United Way 211 Texas
address: 1500 N Main St, Fort Worth, TX 76164
eligibility: Open to all Texans
notes: Free 211 information and referral line. Always relevant for emergency needs.

name: Recovery Resource Council
eligibility: Tarrant County residents needing substance use support
notes: Outpatient substance use treatment, recovery support, prevention.

name: Fort Worth Housing Solutions
eligibility: Income-eligible Fort Worth residents
notes: Housing Choice Voucher and public housing for Fort Worth.

name: Tarrant County Homeless Coalition
eligibility: Persons experiencing homelessness in Tarrant County
notes: Coordinated entry to homeless services. Call to access any HUD-funded shelter.

name: Presbyterian Night Shelter
eligibility: Adults experiencing homelessness
notes: Largest emergency shelter in Tarrant County. Onsite case management and Joining Forces job program.

name: Salvation Army Fort Worth
address: 1855 E Lancaster Ave, Fort Worth, TX 76103
eligibility: Open to all in need
notes: Emergency shelter (Mabee Center), food, utility/rent assistance.

name: Tarrant County College - Workforce Training
address: 300 Trinity Campus Cir, Fort Worth, TX 76102
eligibility: Open to adults; financial aid available
hours: Multiple class schedules; campus hours vary
notes: Industry-aligned credentials. Pell-eligible. Five Tarrant campuses.

name: Workforce Solutions WIOA Training Vouchers
eligibility: Income-eligible adults, dislocated workers, youth
notes: WIOA-funded training vouchers up to $7,500 for industry-recognized credentials.

name: Tarleton State University Workforce Programs
address: 10850 Texan Rider Dr, Fort Worth, TX 76036
eligibility: Open admission for non-credit; admission required for credit
notes: Tarleton's Fort Worth campus offers degree completion and continuing education.

name: GreenPath Financial Wellness
eligibility: Open to all consumers
notes: Free credit counseling and credit report review. Also offers debt management plans.

name: Consumer Credit Counseling Service of Greater DFW
address: 9221 Lyndon B Johnson Fwy, Dallas, TX 75243
eligibility: Open to consumers in credit distress
notes: Nonprofit credit counseling for DFW metro. HUD-approved housing counselor.

name: United Way - Earn It Keep It Save It (VITA)
address: 1500 N Main St, Fort Worth, TX 76164
eligibility: Households earning under $66,000
notes: Free IRS-certified tax prep for low-income households. Boosts EITC claims.

name: Tarrant Area Food Bank
eligibility: Open to all in need
notes: Distributes food to 400+ partner pantries across Tarrant County. Mobile pantry schedule online.

name: Community Food Bank Fort Worth
eligibility: Open to households needing food assistance
notes: Walk-in pantry. No appointment needed. Fresh produce and pantry staples.

name: Mental Health Connection of Tarrant County
address: 1701 River Run Suite 1010, Fort Worth, TX 76107
eligibility: Tarrant County residents
notes: Mental health system navigation and advocacy. Not direct service — referral and education.

name: AARP Senior Community Service Employment
address: 1200 Circle Dr Suite 200, Fort Worth, TX 76119
eligibility: Adults 55+ at or below 125% FPL
notes: Paid training program for low-income adults 55+. Co-located with Workforce Solutions.

name: Workforce Solutions for Tarrant County - General Job Search
eligibility: Open to all jobseekers
notes: TWC anchor for Tarrant County. General services for any active jobseeker.

name: TWC Training Programs - Tarrant County (WIOA Vouchers)
eligibility: Income-eligible per WIOA guidelines; in-demand occupation training only
notes: WIOA-funded training vouchers up to $7,500 for in-demand fields (CNA, CDL, welding, IT).

name: TWC Transportation Vouchers - Tarrant County
eligibility: Active jobseekers in WIOA / Choices / SNAP E&T programs
notes: Gas cards or Trinity Metro bus passes for active jobseekers in supported workforce programs.

name: TWC Reentry Program - Tarrant County
eligibility: Adults with criminal records; recently released or on parole/probation
notes: Dedicated reentry navigators on site. Connections to fair-chance employers (Goodwill, BNSF, Trinity Metro, JPS dietary).

name: TWC Child Care Subsidy - Tarrant County
eligibility: Working / training parents under 85% State Median Income
notes: Child care subsidy for working or training parents. Apply online or in person.

name: Trinity Metro Bus - Job Corridor Routes
address: 801 E Northside Dr, Fort Worth, TX 76102
eligibility: Open to all riders; reduced fare available
notes: Major Fort Worth bus routes covering downtown, hospitals, AllianceTexas, and major employer corridors.

name: TEXRail - Fort Worth to DFW Airport
eligibility: Open to all riders
notes: Commuter rail Fort Worth ITC <-> DFW Airport via Grapevine. Connects to TRE for Dallas.

name: Trinity Metro ZIPZONE On-Demand Microtransit
address: 801 E Northside Dr, Fort Worth, TX 76102
eligibility: Riders within ZIPZONE service areas
notes: On-demand shared rides within 5 Fort Worth zones. Same Trinity Metro fare as bus.

name: ACCESS Trinity Metro - ADA Paratransit
address: 801 E Northside Dr, Fort Worth, TX 76102
eligibility: ADA-eligible riders unable to use fixed-route bus
notes: Curb-to-curb paratransit service for ADA-eligible riders. Apply with documentation.

name: Catholic Charities Fort Worth - Gas Vouchers + Auto Repair
address: 249 W Thornhill Dr, Fort Worth, TX 76115
eligibility: Income-eligible (under 200% FPL); case-managed clients
notes: Gas vouchers and one-time car repair grants up to $1,500 for case-managed clients.

name: TANF Transportation Assistance
address: Texas Health and Human Services - Tarrant County
eligibility: TANF recipients with employment / training plans
notes: TANF work-requirement participants can request transportation assistance through their case worker.

name: Tarrant County Reentry Bus Pass Program
eligibility: Tarrant County residents recently released from incarceration / on community supervision
notes: 30-day Trinity Metro passes for jobseekers in Tarrant County reentry programs.

name: AirCheck Texas - Drive a Clean Machine
address: Statewide TCEQ program - Fort Worth field office
eligibility: Vehicle failed emissions in Tarrant / Dallas / Collin / Denton / Ellis / Johnson / Kaufman / Parker / Rockwall counties; income-eligible
notes: TCEQ emissions repair grants up to $600 + replacement assistance up to $3,500 for low-income who fail emissions.

name: Cars 4 Christmas Tarrant County
eligibility: Single parents, working poor, those overcoming hardship; nominated each fall
hours: Applications accepted each October; vehicles given Christmas Eve
notes: Annual vehicle giveaway program. Apply October each year, vehicles given Christmas Eve.

name: Trinity Habitat for Humanity - Vehicle Programs
address: 9333 N Normandale St, Fort Worth, TX 76116
eligibility: Low-income families; partnership with car repair / donation programs
notes: Occasional vehicle grant programs through Habitat partnerships. Always confirm current availability.

name: ModivCare - Medicaid Non-Emergency Medical Transport
eligibility: Texas Medicaid recipients with documented medical appointments
hours: Schedule 24-72 hours in advance; service Mon-Sun 24/7
notes: Free transportation to medical appointments for Medicaid recipients statewide.

name: Texas Health Steps Transportation
eligibility: Children on Medicaid (under 21) with medical appointments
notes: Medical transportation for Medicaid-enrolled children to well-child visits and specialist appointments.

name: Tarrant Area Agency on Aging - Senior Transportation
eligibility: Adults 60+ in Tarrant County
notes: Free / low-cost transportation for Tarrant County seniors to medical and senior services.

name: MITS - Mobility Impaired Transportation Service
eligibility: Tarrant County residents with mobility impairments
notes: Transportation for Tarrant County residents with mobility impairments. Advance scheduling required.

name: Texas DPS Driver License Office - Fort Worth
address: 1408 W Mitchell St, Fort Worth, TX 76104
eligibility: Texas residents needing license / ID services
notes: Closest central DPS office. License reinstatement requires resolved holds and applicable fees.

name: Operation Reentry Texas - License Reinstatement
eligibility: Adults with criminal records facing license suspensions tied to court fees / fines
notes: Nonprofit helping ex-offenders navigate driver license reinstatement and Driver Responsibility Program holds.

name: IDignity Fort Worth
eligibility: Homeless or very low-income adults needing ID
hours: Quarterly clinics; check website for next event
notes: Quarterly ID restoration clinics for homeless / very low-income jobseekers. Helps gather birth certificate, ID, SSN.

name: Trinity Metro Bike - Fort Worth Bike Share
eligibility: Open to all riders; discounted memberships for low-income
notes: Bike share with low-income membership discount. Useful for last-mile commute from Trinity Metro stops.

name: Recycle Bicycles for Kids - Fort Worth
eligibility: Low-income families and adults referred by partner agencies
notes: Refurbished bike giveaway events. Useful for car-free commute or kids who need transport to school.

name: 211 Texas / United Way 211 Tarrant County - Transportation Referrals
eligibility: All Tarrant County residents
notes: Call 211 for personalized referrals to transportation, food, housing, medical, and crisis resources.

name: Texas Veterans Commission - Fort Worth Field Office
address: 1300 S Hampton Rd, Dallas, TX 75208 (Fort Worth field staff)
eligibility: Texas veterans and their families
notes: TVC counselors help with VA disability claims, employment, education benefits.

name: U.S. VETS - Fort Worth
address: Fort Worth, TX (call for clinic location)
eligibility: Veterans experiencing homelessness or at risk
notes: Transitional housing and employment services for at-risk veterans.

name: SafeHaven of Tarrant County
eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence and their children
notes: Largest DV shelter in Tarrant County. 24-hour confidential hotline.

name: Women's Center of Tarrant County
eligibility: Women, children, and families in crisis
notes: Sexual assault and rape crisis services. Counseling and employment support.

name: REACH Resource Center on Independent Living - Fort Worth
eligibility: People with disabilities seeking independent living support
notes: Centers for Independent Living model. Peer support and advocacy for disability community.

name: Fort Worth Pride Center
address: 1407 W Magnolia Ave, Fort Worth, TX 76104
eligibility: LGBTQ+ community members and allies
notes: Community center for Fort Worth's LGBTQ+ community. Support groups and case management.

name: Catholic Charities Fort Worth - Refugee Services
address: 249 W Thornhill Dr, Fort Worth, TX 76115
eligibility: Refugees, asylees, special immigrant visa holders
notes: Federal refugee resettlement contractor. ESL, employment, immigration services.

name: Tarrant Area Agency on Aging - Standalone Aging Services
eligibility: Adults 60+ in Tarrant County
notes: Federally designated AAA for Tarrant. Benefits counseling, caregiver support, ombudsman.

name: Senior Citizens Services of Greater Tarrant County
eligibility: Adults 60+ in Tarrant County
notes: Meals on Wheels and senior center programming for Tarrant County seniors.

name: Big Brothers Big Sisters Lone Star
address: 8920 Stemmons Fwy, Dallas, TX 75247 (serving Fort Worth)
eligibility: Children ages 6-18 in single-parent or low-income households
notes: 1-on-1 youth mentoring for kids in Fort Worth and Tarrant County.

name: YMCA of Metropolitan Fort Worth
eligibility: All ages; financial assistance available
notes: Fort Worth YMCAs offer after-school care, summer camps, scholarships available.

name: Recovery Resource Council Fort Worth
eligibility: Adults and youth seeking substance use recovery; sliding scale
notes: Substance use counseling, MAT referrals, sliding-scale fees.

name: Alcoholics Anonymous - District 70 Fort Worth
eligibility: Open to anyone with a desire to stop drinking
hours: Meetings 24/7 across Fort Worth area; helpline 24/7
notes: Free 12-step meetings throughout Fort Worth and Tarrant County.
